descript.abstract: Hydro-flattened breaklines were compiled and followed the Quality Level Two (QL2) standards as outlined in the USGS-NGP Lidar Base Specifications, Techniques and Methods 11-B4, Version 1.2, November 2014 (TM11-B4). Rivers greater than 100 feet wide and waterbodies larger than 2 square acres.
descript.purpose: LiDAR terrain mapping to the Colorado Water Conservation Board (CWCB) for all or portions of Garfield, Eagle, Summit, Clear Creek, Gilpin, Douglas and Jefferson Counties. Approximately 6,150 square miles.

idinfo.native: Microsoft Windows Vista Version 6.1 (Build 7601) Service Pack 1; ESRI ArcCatalog 9.3.1.4095

attracc.attraccr: Verification of attribute accuracy for LiDAR data involves the use of filtering techniques. Auto-filtering uses algorithms to reclassify data in the point cloud. Hand-filtering involves the analysis of a technician working in a graphic environment to reclassify the erroneous points that may remain in the LiDAR point cloud after auto-filter.

dataqual.logic: Calibrations of the LiDAR flightline data are performed; this process is known as boresighting. The flightlines are compared to evaluate both their relative and absolute accuracies.
dataqual.complete: The dataset was reviewed to ensure completeness and accuracy.

horizpa.horizpar: This data set was produced to meet ASPRS Positional Accuracy Standards for Digital Geospatial Data (2014) for a 42.4cm RMSEx / RMSEy Horizontal Accuracy Class which equates to Positional Horizontal Accuracy = +/- 73.4cm at a 95% confidence level.

vertacc.vertaccr: This data set was tested to meet ASPRS Positional Accuracy Standard for Digital Geospatial Data (2014) for a 10.0-cm RMSEz Vertical Accuracy. 37 NVA check points, 38 VVA check points Class. Actual NVA accuracy was found to be RMSEz = 4.98cm, equating to +/- 9.77cm at the 95% confidence level. Actual VVA accuracy was found to be +/- 12.37cm at the 95th percentile.

srcinfo.srccontr: 37 NVA check points, 38 VVA check points were used.

procstep.procdesc: Using MARS software hydro-flattened breaklines (rivers greater than 100 feet wide and waterbodies greater than two square acres) were compiled. MARS software using Intensity values and 3d elevations generates ESRI shape files which are then imported into an ESRI gdb for a project wide delivery. Monotonicity checks where validated (water flowing downstream).

altsys.altenc: Explicit elevation coordinate included with horizontal coordinates

attrdomv.udom: Sequential unique whole numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: Length of feature in internal units.

attrdomv.udom: Positive real numbers that are automatically generated.

overview.eaover: Rivers greater than 100 feet across Waterbodies greater than 2 square acres
overview.eadetcit: Rivers greater than 100 feet across Waterbodies greater than 2 square acres

metainfo.metstdn: FGDC Content Standards for Digital Geospatial Metadata
